title: Nursery Rhyme. XCIV. Proverbs.
content:
[The following is quoted in Miege's 'Great French Dictionary,' fol. Lond. 1687, 2d part.]

        A swarm of bees in May
        Is worth a load of hay;
        A swarm of bees in June
        Is worth a silver spoon;
        A swarm of bees in July
        Is not worth a fly.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. XCIV. Proverbs.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
